newgame.sketchHint: (black ink on sepia parchment — costs a few extra credits per turn)
newgame.sketchQuestion: Generate an AI scene sketch after each turn?

describe.armorNone: little more than the clothes on your back

loop.authFail: Still failing after re-authentication. Use /settings to update your API key.
loop.gmUnavailable: The Game Master was not available. Try again when you are ready.
loop.turnFail: The turn was not resolved. Try again or type /restart.

victory.text: You have found {{treasure}} and made it out alive. The adventure ends in triumph — for now.

defeat.text: The world dims. Grizzik's mocking cackle echoes through the stone as you collapse to the cold floor. Your adventure ends here — for now.

entrance.name: Entrance Hall
entrance.desc: You stand in the entrance hall of a {{style}}. Tattered tapestries cling to damp stone walls, and the reek of mildew fills your lungs.

entrance.name: Foyer
entrance.desc: The foyer of this {{style}} greets you with warped floorboards and the ghost of old grandeur. A cracked mirror reflects your wary face.

entrance.name: Mudroom
entrance.desc: A narrow mudroom at the threshold of the {{style}}. Boots long rotted away line one wall; rusted coat-hooks the other.

entrance.name: Gatehouse
entrance.desc: A low-ceilinged gatehouse opens before you, its iron portcullis rusted halfway up. Beyond it the {{style}} waits in silence.

entrance.name: Threshold
entrance.desc: Cracked flagstones mark the threshold of the {{style}}. A cold draught carries the scent of damp earth and something older.

hall.name: Great Hall
hall.desc: A vaulted great hall stretches before you, its long feasting table overturned. Moonlight bleeds through a cracked skylight above.

hall.name: Drawing Room
hall.desc: The drawing room reeks of old smoke and something worse. Furniture has been shoved to the walls as if barricaded, then abandoned.

hall.name: Banquet Chamber
hall.desc: A vast banquet chamber with a chandelier hanging crooked from a single chain. Plates still sit on the table — a feast interrupted decades ago.

hall.name: Gallery
hall.desc: A long gallery lined with empty portrait frames. The paintings are gone, but faint outlines on the walls hint at what once hung here.

hall.name: Audience Room
hall.desc: A raised dais at one end suggests this was once an audience room. The chair atop it has been smashed to kindling.

corridor.name: Stone Corridor
corridor.desc: A long corridor bisects the heart of the building. Sconces hold nothing but wax stumps. Your footsteps echo off the bare stone.

corridor.name: Narrow Passage
corridor.desc: A narrow passage barely wide enough for one. The walls press close and the air is stale. Something scratches faintly in the dark ahead.

corridor.name: Arched Hallway
corridor.desc: An arched hallway with crumbling plaster revealing rough brick beneath. Cobwebs hang in curtains from the vaulting.

corridor.name: Servants' Passage
corridor.desc: A hidden servants' passage, low and cramped. Hooks on the walls once held lanterns; now they hold only dust.

corridor.name: Cellar Stairs
corridor.desc: Worn stone steps spiral downward, slick with moisture. The temperature drops with each step, and the walls glisten.

chamber.name: Dusty Chamber
chamber.desc: A square chamber thick with dust. Footprints other than your own cross the floor — someone was here recently.

chamber.name: Round Room
chamber.desc: A curious round room with a domed ceiling. Acoustic tricks make every sound reverberate — even your breathing feels amplified.

chamber.name: Antechamber
chamber.desc: A small antechamber with a mosaic floor, most tiles cracked or missing. What remains shows a serpent eating its own tail.

chamber.name: Guard Room
chamber.desc: A guard room with weapon racks mounted on every wall — all empty. A table holds dice and the remnants of an interrupted card game.

chamber.name: Cold Room
chamber.desc: An unnaturally cold room. Your breath clouds in front of you. Frost rimes the corners and the stone feels like ice through your boots.

storage.name: Kitchen
storage.desc: The kitchen still holds the ghost of old meals. Pots hang from rusted hooks, and a butcher's block is scarred deep by years of use.

storage.name: Pantry
storage.desc: Rows of empty shelves line the pantry, their contents long since spoiled or stolen. The air is thick with the memory of spice.

storage.name: Wine Cellar
storage.desc: Broken casks line the walls of this wine cellar. A dark stain covers the floor — wine or blood, hard to tell. One barrel remains sealed.

storage.name: Armoury
storage.desc: An armoury picked nearly clean. Armour stands hold nothing but rust-shaped outlines. A single dented shield leans forgotten in a corner.

storage.name: Storeroom
storage.desc: A cluttered storeroom with crates stacked to the ceiling. Most are empty; a few hold mouldy cloth or tarnished buckles.

quarters.name: Servants' Quarters
quarters.desc: A cramped room once shared by servants, now empty of all comfort. Bare pallets are pushed against the walls.

quarters.name: Bedchamber
quarters.desc: A bedchamber with a four-poster frame — the mattress and curtains long gone. Scratches on the bedpost count the days someone was trapped here.

quarters.name: Study
quarters.desc: A small study with a writing desk and a chair. Ink has dried in the well. A half-written letter sits on the desk, the words too faded to read.

quarters.name: Nursery
quarters.desc: A nursery with a rocking crib in the corner. Toys are scattered on the floor — wooden soldiers and a cloth doll with no face.

quarters.name: Monk's Cell
quarters.desc: A bare monk's cell: stone bed, stone floor, stone walls. Someone scratched a prayer into the wall with their fingernails.

shrine.name: Ruined Chapel
shrine.desc: A small chapel with pews overturned and an altar defaced. Candle stubs still ring the broken altar — someone prayed here not long ago.

shrine.name: Crypt
shrine.desc: A crypt lined with niches holding stone coffins. Most are sealed. One is open — and empty. Grave dust coats everything.

shrine.name: Ritual Chamber
shrine.desc: Arcane symbols cover every surface of this chamber. A circle of melted candles surrounds a dark stain in the centre.

shrine.name: Ossuary
shrine.desc: Bones stacked floor to ceiling in neat rows — skulls arranged in geometric patterns. The air is dry and carries a mineral tang.

shrine.name: Shrine
shrine.desc: A small shrine to a forgotten deity. The idol's face has been chiselled away. Offerings of tarnished coin sit in a stone bowl.

vault.name: Master Study
vault.desc: The master's study is in disarray — shelves ransacked, papers scattered. Yet in the centre of the room, {{treasure}} sits untouched, as though protected by some old ward. A way out beckons.

vault.name: Trophy Room
vault.desc: Glass cases line the trophy room, most shattered and empty. But one pedestal still bears its prize: {{treasure}}. A back exit stands ajar.

vault.name: Vault
vault.desc: Stone walls and an iron-banded floor mark this as the vault proper. The most valuable thing was left behind: {{treasure}}. A passage leads out.

vault.name: Hidden Chamber
vault.desc: A concealed chamber behind a false wall. Cobwebs part to reveal {{treasure}}, resting on a velvet cushion. Light filters in from a crack in the ceiling — freedom.

vault.name: Treasury
vault.desc: A small treasury with iron-bound chests — all empty save one. Inside: {{treasure}}. A drainage grate in the floor leads to open air.

enemies.name: Grizzik the Goblin
enemies.intro: A goblin crouches in the shadows, yellow eyes snapping open as you enter. It snatches up a battered scimitar and bares its teeth: "This place belongs to Grizzik! Turn back or bleed, big-folk!"

enemies.name: Guard Skeleton
enemies.intro: Bones rattle as a skeleton lurches upright from the corner. Its hollow eye sockets fix on you; a rusted longsword rises into a fighting stance with terrible purpose.

enemies.name: Feral Cultist
enemies.intro: A robed figure spins to face you, madness bright in its eyes. It has been waiting here for a reason you don't yet understand — and it levels a long dagger at your throat.

enemies.name: Giant Rat
enemies.intro: A rat the size of a terrier erupts from beneath the debris, hackles raised and yellow teeth bared. It lunges before you can take a breath.

enemies.name: Shambling Corpse
enemies.intro: Something rises from the floor — a corpse in rusted armour, joints cracking as it stands. Its jaw hangs loose and a guttural moan escapes what remains of its throat.

enemies.name: Cave Spider
enemies.intro: Silk threads catch the light a moment before you see the spider — bloated, black-legged, and fast. It drops from the ceiling with a wet hiss.

keys.name: brass key
keys.desc: A tarnished brass key, its bow cast in the shape of a crescent moon.

keys.name: iron key
keys.desc: A heavy iron key, cold to the touch and etched with a single Roman numeral.

keys.name: silver key
keys.desc: A slender silver key that catches the light with an almost warm glow.

keys.name: bone key
keys.desc: A key carved from a single piece of bone — its origin better left unasked.

treasures.name: chest of gold coins
treasures.desc: A brass-banded chest overflowing with gold coins, worth a small fortune.

treasures.name: sapphire amulet
treasures.desc: A deep-blue sapphire set in filigreed silver, pulsing with faint inner light.

treasures.name: sealed arcane tome
treasures.desc: A thick book sealed with wax and cord, its cover warm despite the cold room.

treasures.name: jewelled ceremonial sword
treasures.desc: A sword more art than weapon — its hilt crusted with rubies and engraved silver.

loot.name: healing potion
loot.desc: A small glass vial of red liquid that sloshes with a faint warmth.

loot.name: silver ring
loot.desc: A plain silver ring engraved with tiny runes on the inside band.

loot.name: old journal
loot.desc: A leather-bound journal filled with frantic handwriting. The last entry is unfinished.

loot.name: handful of coins
loot.desc: A scatter of copper and silver coins, perhaps ten in all.

chips.unlockCmd: I use the key to unlock the door

athletics.label: Athletics
athletics.desc: Climb, jump, swim, or grapple. Raw physical effort against resistance.

acrobatics.label: Acrobatics
acrobatics.desc: Balance, tumble, or escape a grapple. Finesse and body control.

sleight-of-hand.label: Sleight of Hand
sleight-of-hand.desc: Pick pockets, plant objects, or perform manual trickery unseen.

stealth.label: Stealth
stealth.desc: Move silently and stay hidden. Opposed by passive Perception.

arcana.label: Arcana
arcana.desc: Recall lore about spells, magic items, and the planes.

history.label: History
history.desc: Recall past events, legendary figures, and ancient civilisations.

investigation.label: Investigation
investigation.desc: Search for clues, find hidden doors, or deduce what happened.

nature.label: Nature
nature.desc: Identify plants, animals, weather patterns, and natural hazards.

religion.label: Religion
religion.desc: Recall lore about deities, rites, cults, and holy symbols.

animal-handling.label: Animal Handling
animal-handling.desc: Calm, guide, or read the intent of beasts and mounts.

insight.label: Insight
insight.desc: Read someone's true feelings or detect when they're lying.

medicine.label: Medicine
medicine.desc: Stabilise a dying creature, diagnose ailments, or tend wounds.

perception.label: Perception
perception.desc: Notice threats, spot hidden creatures, or hear distant sounds.

survival.label: Survival
survival.desc: Track prey, forage food, navigate terrain, or endure the wild.

deception.label: Deception
deception.desc: Lie convincingly, disguise your intent, or create a false impression.

intimidation.label: Intimidation
intimidation.desc: Coerce through threats, menace, or sheer force of presence.

performance.label: Performance
performance.desc: Entertain, impersonate, or captivate an audience.

persuasion.label: Persuasion
persuasion.desc: Win someone over through charm, reasoned argument, or diplomacy.

secondWind.label: Second Wind
secondWind.note:
Bonus action: regain 1d10+{{lvl}} HP.
Once per short rest.
secondWind.text: I use Second Wind to heal myself

actionSurge.label: Action Surge
actionSurge.note:
Take one additional action this turn.
Once per short rest.
actionSurge.text: I use Action Surge for an extra action

sneakAttack.label: Sneak Attack
sneakAttack.note:
Deal {{dice}}d6 extra damage when you have advantage
or an ally flanks your target.

cunningAction.label: Cunning Action
cunningAction.note:
Bonus action: Dash, Disengage, or Hide.
Keeps you mobile without spending your main action.

turnUndead.label: Turn Undead
turnUndead.note:
Channel Divinity: undead within 30 ft must flee.
WIS save DC {{dc}} or be turned for 1 minute.
turnUndead.text: I use Channel Divinity: Turn Undead

castSpell.label: Cast Spell
castSpell.note:
Cast a prepared spell. Targets resist with DC {{dc}}.
Concentration spells last until broken or you cast another.

arcaneRecovery.label: Arcane Recovery
arcaneRecovery.note:
Short rest: regain spell slots up to level {{slotLvl}}.
Once per long rest.

castSpell.label: Cast Spell
castSpell.note:
Cast a prepared spell. +{{atk}} to spell attack rolls.
Higher spell slots deal more damage or last longer.

exports.noJournal: Nothing to export yet — play a few turns first.
exports.craftFail: Could not generate story — exporting raw journal instead.
exports.craftFallback: Falling back to raw HTML journal.
exports.noSketch: No scene sketch to export yet.
exports.noSketches: No sketches generated this session yet.
exports.importFail: Failed to import — file is not a valid save.
exports.subtitle: A {{class}} — Dan's Dungeons

ai.classifierPrompt:
You are a D&D action classifier. Classify the player's intent from their free-text input.

The player writes in English.

Current scene (use this to identify valid targets):
{{scene}}

Output a single JSON object. Be generous in interpretation ("hit the goblin" → attack, "sneak past" → skill stealth).
- "go north" / "head through the doorway" → move (set direction to north/south/east/west)
- "take the key" / "grab the brass key"  → take (set target_id to the item's id from scene loot)
- "unlock the door" / "use the key"       → unlock
- direction: the cardinal direction for move, null otherwise
- target_id: NPC id for attack, item id for take, null otherwise
For skill checks suggest a dc between 10 and 20.
ai.narratorPrompt:
You are the Game Master narrating a D&D 5e dungeon encounter.

Setting: gritty low fantasy, second person ("you strike", "you dodge").
Narrate in English.

Rules:
- Do NOT invent dice results — use only the resolved facts in the data
- 2–4 sentences; vivid but concise
- If a hit: describe the impact and the enemy's reaction
- If a miss: describe the near-miss
- If the enemy retaliated: weave it into the same narration
- End on tension or consequence
- If intent is 'move': describe entering the new room (use newRoom.description); introduce any enemies using their intro text; mention visible items
- If intent is 'take': describe picking up the item
- If intent is 'unlock': describe unlocking the door with a satisfying click
- CRITICAL — if intent is 'impossible': the action simply cannot happen. Do NOT describe it succeeding or partially succeeding. Describe only the failure and its reason. No enemy dies, no item is taken, nothing changes.

Recent transcript (last 3 turns, for continuity):
{{transcript}}

Current scene:
{{scene}}

Resolved mechanics:
{{resolved}}

Output ONLY a JSON object — no markdown, no extra text:
{"narration":"...","combat_ended":true/false,"outcome":"continue"|"victory"|"defeat"|"flee"}
ai.narrateTurnPrompt: Narrate the outcome of this turn.
ai.autoplayPrompt:
You are a bold, curious adventurer playing a D&D dungeon crawl. You act with personality — you're not a robot picking from a menu. Think like a real player who wants to have fun.

Personality:
- Curious and thorough: investigate rooms, examine interesting details, talk to NPCs before fighting
- Combat with flair: use your class abilities and weapon attacks creatively, not just "I attack" — describe what you do ("I lunge at the goblin with my longsword", "I cast a spell at the skeleton")
- Never backtrack: always push forward into unexplored areas. NEVER go back to a room you've already visited unless there's no other option
- Resourceful: pick up items, use keys on locked doors, leverage your skills
- Brave but not stupid: if HP is low, use healing abilities (Second Wind, etc.) or play defensively

Decision priority:
1. If there's a hostile enemy in the room — fight it using your best weapon or class ability
2. If there are items on the ground — pick them up
3. If there's a locked door and you have a key — unlock it
4. Explore an exit you haven't been through yet (check the transcript to avoid revisiting rooms)
5. Use a skill that fits the situation (Perception to spot hidden things, Stealth to sneak, Investigation to search)
6. Look around or wait ONLY if nothing else makes sense

IMPORTANT: Check the recent transcript carefully — do NOT repeat the same action twice in a row, and do NOT go back to rooms you came from.

Current scene:
{{scene}}

Available actions:
{{actions}}

Recent transcript (use this to avoid repeating actions and backtracking):
{{transcript}}

Respond in English. Output ONLY a JSON object:
{"action": "a natural, in-character action sentence"}
ai.journalPrompt:
You are a fantasy author retelling a D&D adventure as a coherent, immersive tale.

Character: {{name}}, a {{class}}.

Write in second person past tense ("You drew your blade…"). Weave the turn-by-turn narrations below into flowing literary prose — not a transcript, but a proper story with atmosphere, tension, and rhythm.

Rules:
- Create chapter breaks at natural narrative beats (entering a new room, a combat encounter, a discovery)
- Each chapter needs a short evocative heading (e.g. "The Hall of Broken Tables", "Steel Against Bone")
- Add sensory details, transitions between scenes, and emotional beats
- Preserve all key events and outcomes from the narrations — do not invent new plot points
- Open with atmosphere, close with weight or reflection
- Keep the gritty low-fantasy tone

Narrations to weave into the tale:
{{narrations}}{{context}}

Output ONLY a JSON object:
{"title": "an evocative story title", "chapters": [{"heading": "chapter heading", "text": "the prose"}, ...]}
ai.worldSeedPrompt:
You are a fantasy world architect. Create a unique D&D world with a central dramatic conflict.

Generate:
- A world name (evocative, 1-2 words)
- A tone: grimdark, heroic, or mysterious
- A creation myth (2 sentences max)
- 2-3 gods with name and domain
- A red thread: a central dramatic premise (the overarching conflict) and a starting hook (what the player first encounters)
- A digest: a compressed ~30-word summary of the above for injecting into child generators

Be original. Avoid generic fantasy tropes. The world should feel lived-in and specific.

Output ONLY a JSON object matching the schema.
ai.factionsPrompt:
You are defining the political factions of a fantasy world. Given the world digest below, create 2-3 factions that shape the world's power dynamics.

World digest:
{{parentDigest}}

For each faction generate:
- id (slug: faction-name)
- name (evocative, 2-4 words)
- description (what they stand for, 1-2 sentences)
- values (comma-separated: e.g. "order, tradition, secrecy")
- allies: array of other faction ids they cooperate with (can be empty)
- enemies: array of other faction ids they oppose (at least one faction should have an enemy)
- territory: array of region ids they control (empty for now — will be filled as regions generate)
- digest: ~20-word summary

Make the factions feel like they have genuine tension with each other. At least two factions should be in conflict.

Output ONLY a JSON object: {"factions": [...]}
ai.regionPrompt:
You are expanding a fantasy world. Given the world digest below, create a starting region (country/territory).

World digest:
{{parentDigest}}

Generate:
- A region id (slug format: region-name)
- Region name, climate, and a vivid 2-3 sentence description
- The name of the main settlement (village or town)
- The name of a dungeon/ruin/cave nearby
- A rumor the locals whisper that connects to the world's red thread
- 2-3 hints about what lies in adjacent regions (e.g. "dense forest to the north")
- A digest: ~40-word summary of this region for child generators

Output ONLY a JSON object matching the schema.
ai.settlementPrompt:
You are fleshing out a settlement in a fantasy world. Given the region digest below, create a village or town.

Region digest:
{{parentDigest}}

Generate:
- Settlement id (slug: settlement-name) and regionId: "{{regionId}}"
- Name and a vivid 2-3 sentence description
- 3-4 NPCs, each with:
  - id (slug: npc-name), name, role (innkeeper/questgiver/merchant/guard/elder/blacksmith/healer/hermit)
  - attitude (friendly/neutral/suspicious/hostile)
  - greeting (their opening line when the player talks to them)
  - questHook (string or null — the questgiver MUST have one pointing to the dungeon)
  - personality (a vivid 2-3 word description: "gruff but kind", "paranoid whisperer", "cheerful drunk")
  - secret (something they know or hide — null if none. e.g. "saw the cult enter the crypt at midnight")
  - factionId (null for v1, will be wired to factions later)
  - relationships: array of {targetId, type} where type is spouse/parent/child/rival/ally/employer/mentor. At least 1 NPC should have a relationship to another NPC in this settlement.
  - inventory: array of {name, price, description} for merchants, null for others. Merchants should have 3-5 items.
- 2-3 exits: direction, target name, target type (dungeon/road/wilderness), targetId (slug for dungeons, null for roads)
- A digest: ~30-word summary for child generators

Make NPCs feel real: give them personality, secrets, and connections to each other.

Output ONLY a JSON object matching the schema.
